extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 38km remaining on the penultimate Cat 1 climb, soloed to victory and moved into podium contention while VALVERDE's late chase isolated ROGLIČ.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR's 38km solo attack exploited hesitation when rivals expected him to wait for final 10km ramps.
VALVERDE's late chase served dual purpose: limit POGAČAR's podium threat and test ROGLIČ without actually attacking overall lead.
LÓPEZ completely passive when dropped, showing UAE successfully boxed Movistar by placing breakaway riders ahead preventing team chase.
ROGLIČ marked VALVERDE's wheel but refused to share workload, prioritizing GC defense over limiting POGAČAR's stage gap.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: 20-year-old executed 38km solo raid with descending skill and tactical maturity beyond his age, securing white jersey
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: late acceleration aimed at podium defense against POGAČAR rather than GC challenge, ROGLIČ neutralized by sitting wheel
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: tactically disciplined refusal to chase VALVERDE preserved 2:33 GC lead despite stage loss
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: completely passive when dropped, showed no response capacity when UAE Emirates controlled front breakaway composition
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: slipped to 4th overall after being unable to follow VALVERDE's late acceleration for podium defense
